text.title: pytest-django Configuration and Fixtures
text.summary: pyproject.toml settings, directory layout, shared conftest fixtures, and factory_boy pattern.

rule.statement: Set DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE in pyproject.toml [tool.pytest.ini_options] to a test-specific settings file with DEBUG=False, in-memory cache, fake email backend, and MD5PasswordHasher.
rule.rationale: Test settings reduce overhead and prevent accidental real email/payment sends. MD5PasswordHasher speeds up tests by 10-100x but must never leak into dev/prod settings.

example.code: [tool.pytest.ini_options] DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE = "config.settings.test" python_files = ["test_*.py"] python_functions = ["test_*"] addopts = ["-v", "--tb=short", "--strict-markers", "-ra"] markers = [ "slow: deselect with '-m not slow'", "integration: integration tests", ]

rule.statement: Keep conftest.py close to where fixtures are used. Project-root conftest only for truly global fixtures (api_client, user, auth_client, network-blocking).
rule.rationale: Auto-discovery cascades — nested conftest fixtures shadow each other silently if not scoped.

section.title: Shared Fixtures

rule.statement: Add an autouse network-blocking fixture in the root conftest that raises on any socket.create_connection call. Tests that need real network must explicitly opt out.
rule.rationale: Tests that forget to mock external services produce 30s timeouts, not clear failures. A loud guard makes the gap obvious immediately.

section.reference: Minimal conftest with api_client, user, auth_client, and network-blocking fixture.

section.title: Factory Pattern

rule.statement: Use factory_boy DjangoModelFactory instead of fixture-as-function for test data. Override fields per test with UserFactory(email='x@y.z').
rule.rationale: factory_boy handles sequences and related objects; plain fixtures-as-functions require manual wiring for each variant.

section.reference: DjangoModelFactory scaffolds for User and related models.

example.code:
import factory from apps.users.models import User
class UserFactory(factory.django.DjangoModelFactory): class Meta: model = User email = factory.Sequence(lambda n: f"user{n}@example.com") name = factory.Faker("name")
